content:
Key Findings

  • Preston City Council's CAP DEVELOPMENT department spent £43.9M over seven years, accounting for 21.3% of total spending.
  • The second-highest spending department, REV CUSTOMER SERVICES, spent £30.9M, which is £13M less than CAP DEVELOPMENT.
  • CAP DEVELOPMENT spending is more than double that of CAP CUSTOMER SERVICES, which spent £23.4M.
  • REV ENVIRONMENT and REV DEVELOPMENT spent £28.4M and £19.7M respectively.
  • The CAP CHIEF EXECUTIVE department spent the least, with £3.3M over the period.

CAP DEVELOPMENT: Preston's Largest Spending Department

Preston City Council's CAP DEVELOPMENT department stands out as the largest spending department, with a total expenditure of £43.9M over seven years. This amount represents 21.3% of the council's total spending of £205.9M, highlighting its significant role in the council's financial operations. The department's spending is more than double that of the next largest department, REV CUSTOMER SERVICES, which spent £30.9M.

The substantial spending by CAP DEVELOPMENT suggests a focus on capital projects and infrastructure development. This could include investments in housing, transportation, and other long-term assets that support the city's growth and development. The department's expenditure is a critical area for residents to understand, as it directly impacts the city's infrastructure and services.

Comparing Departmental Spending

The CAP DEVELOPMENT department's spending of £43.9M is significantly higher than other departments. For instance, REV ENVIRONMENT spent £28.4M, which is £15.5M less than CAP DEVELOPMENT. Similarly, CAP CUSTOMER SERVICES spent £23.4M, which is £20.5M less. This comparison underscores the prioritisation of capital development projects within the council's budget.

The following table provides a detailed breakdown of the top spending departments within Preston City Council:

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 CAP DEVELOPMENT £43.9M 21.3%
2 REV CUSTOMER SERVICES £30.9M 15.0%
3 REV ENVIRONMENT £28.4M 13.8%
4 CAP CUSTOMER SERVICES £23.4M 11.3%
5 REV DEVELOPMENT £19.7M 9.5%

Understanding the Spending Breakdown

The CAP DEVELOPMENT department's spending of £43.9M is a substantial portion of the council's budget. This expenditure likely includes a range of projects aimed at improving the city's infrastructure and services. For example, it could cover investments in housing developments, transportation networks, and public facilities. Understanding the specific projects funded by this department can provide insights into the council's priorities and long-term planning.

Comparatively, the CAP CHIEF EXECUTIVE department spent the least, with £3.3M over the seven-year period. This significant difference in spending highlights the varying roles and responsibilities of different departments within the council. The CAP DEVELOPMENT department's higher expenditure reflects its critical role in shaping the city's physical and economic landscape.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Preston City Council about the specific projects funded by the CAP DEVELOPMENT department.
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about the planning and execution of capital development projects.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see the suppliers and contractors involved in CAP DEVELOPMENT projects.
  • Write to your local councillor to express your views on the priorities and spending of the CAP DEVELOPMENT department.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain a deeper understanding of Preston City Council's financial operations.
This article is based on Preston City Council's published spending data covering 2019/20 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
